nodes.norm_label: task 3: fixture analysis route and sse parser

nodes.label: Pull Request Verification Contract
nodes.rationale: PRs record acceptance, dependency, test, CI, and TDD evidence so readiness is auditable.

nodes.label: Application Scaffold Detection
nodes.rationale: CI remains green while the repository contains only planning docs, then activates application checks as soon as package.json exists.

nodes.label: Vertical Red-Green-Refactor TDD
nodes.rationale: Thin behavior-level cycles preserve fast feedback and discourage production-first implementation.

nodes.label: One Issue One Branch One PR Workflow
nodes.norm_label: one issue one branch one pr workflow

nodes.label: Risk-Based No-Mistakes Gate
nodes.rationale: Expensive autonomous review is reserved for subtle demo-breaking pipeline, scoring, and integration risks rather than routine work.

nodes.label: Canonical Domain Language
nodes.rationale: Code, tests, issues, and PRs use the glossary consistently; meaning changes require an explicit decision.

nodes.label: Architecture Decision Records
nodes.rationale: Only hard-to-reverse surprising tradeoffs warrant durable historical records.

nodes.label: Milestone-Based Graphify Workflow
nodes.source_location: When to use it and Repository policy
nodes.rationale: Architecture graphs are refreshed at contract, core-module, and integration milestones; per-commit rebuilds would slow the hackathon loop.

nodes.label: Isolated Worktree Ownership
nodes.rationale: Each parallel worker gets one issue, branch, worktree, and PR so workers cannot share branches or concurrently mutate a shared contract.

nodes.label: Fixture-Backed Playwright
nodes.rationale: The browser exercises the real route, SSE parser, and rendered UI using deterministic events instead of bypassing integration with a route mock.

nodes.label: Frozen Contract Lane Seam
nodes.rationale: Lane A proves the live adapter produces contracts while Lane B proves the user flow from fixture contracts, without importing each other's internals.

nodes.label: Fixture Mode Production Safety
nodes.rationale: Fixture selection must be impossible in production while still traveling through the same public route as live analysis.

nodes.label: Consumer Trust Visual Hierarchy
nodes.rationale: Score, verdict, strongest citation, and next action are prioritized for phone and projector legibility rather than dense terminal-like presentation.

nodes.label: Original Mobbin Reference Composite
nodes.rationale: CapCheck combines intake focus, truthful progress, verdict-first scoring, and expandable evidence without copying navigation, labels, logos, or exact layouts.

nodes.label: Fixture-to-Live Adapter Architecture
nodes.rationale: A shared validated AnalysisEvent contract lets deterministic SSE fixtures and the future live adapter use the same public route without presentation changes.
